Ordinary · First Dance

Enter the passkey to view.

Ordinary ❤ First Dance

From a first tentative touch to a soaring lift and back to a quiet embrace — two people spend the song finding, testing, and finally choosing each other.

Alex Warren · 21 gapless clips · bar-by-bar · 112 BPM

Source Dance From Home (demo) Covers the whole video, 0:00–2:29 Roles Lead = grey suit · Follow = white gown
Lead-driven Follow features Signature moment
0 / 21 learned · 0%
The clips are continuous — played in order (01 → 21) they rebuild the entire video with no gaps or repeats, each cut on a musical bar line. Every card shows which bars of the song it covers and the lyric you’ll hear. Tick “Got it” as you nail each phrase; progress saves in this browser. Gold cards are the three signature moments (the reach + two lifts). Widen the window and the player grows.
Play-through: tick ▶ Queue on the phrases you want and hit Play queued (bottom bar) to run them back-to-back; or Play all for the whole dance end-to-end.
📝 My notes
Entrance
01
0:00–0:09
✓ LEARNED
Bars 1–4

Walk-on & meet both

instrumental intro

Both walk on from opposite sides and meet in the center of the floor.

🌟 Coaching: Walk in on the music, eyes on each other from the first step. Don't rush to center — the entrance sets the whole tone.

Verse 1
02
0:09–0:18
✓ LEARNED
Bars 5–8

First hold & settle both

This town's lost its faith

Hands join into closed position; settle face-to-face and find the pulse.

🌟 Coaching: Build the frame: her left hand on his shoulder, his right on her shoulder blade. Firm but soft; settle your weight before you move.

03
0:18–0:26
✓ LEARNED
Bars 9–12

Closed-position sway both

So if our time is running out

Gentle side-to-side sway with a slow clockwise drift; small traveling steps.

🌟 Coaching: Sway from the hips, knees soft, weight rolling foot to foot. Stay chest-to-chest with level heads — no bobbing.

Pre-chorus
04
0:26–0:30
✓ LEARNED
Bars 13–14

Open-out & underarm turn follow

…a masterpiece — oh, my, my

He opens her to the "V" on a stationary raised arm; she opens out and turns underneath, back to face him.

🌟 Coaching: He gives a clear, stable raised hand; she keeps her own balance and spots the turn, finishing square to him.

05
0:30–0:35
✓ LEARNED
Bars 15–16

Dress-flourish fan-out follow

I take one look at you

Solo presentation: arms wide, gown spread — a picture beat.

🌟 Coaching: Commit to the shape — arms long, chin up, let the gown do the talking. Hold it a beat so the camera catches it.

06
0:35–0:43
✓ LEARNED
Bars 17–20

Close embrace & sway both

You take me out of the ordinary

Draw back together into a close embrace; sway with small quarter-turns.

🌟 Coaching: Come back together softly, no bump. Rebuild the frame and breathe the phrase — this is the intimate reset.

Chorus 1
07
0:43–0:52
✓ LEARNED
Bars 21–24

Sway with quarter-turns both

On the edge of your knife, stayin' drunk on your vine

Continue the closed sway, rotating a little more as the chorus lands.

🌟 Coaching: Small turns from the standing leg, don't muscle them. Keep the box compact so you stay centered on the floor.

08
0:52–1:00
✓ LEARNED
Bars 25–28

Traveling rotation both

So this world will be ordinary

Semi-closed hold rotating and traveling across the floor; the gown sweeps.

🌟 Coaching: Travel on the strong beats with the gown sweeping; he leads the direction, she trusts the rotation. Smooth, not fast.

09
1:00–1:05
✓ LEARNED
Bars 29–30

Rotation into the line both

Somethin' 'bout you, you can touch me to dust

The rotation gathers and opens toward the big extension.

🌟 Coaching: Gather your momentum and aim it toward the open line coming next — think 'wind up' before the reach.

10
1:05–1:09
✓ LEARNED
Bars 31–32

The reach & lunge (open line) both★ SIGNATURE

The angels up in the clouds are jealous…

Wide two-arm extension into a shared lunge — hold the shape, then reel back in. SIGNATURE POSE.

🌟 Coaching: Commit weight into the counterbalance and breathe out on the extension; eyes reach past your fingertips, not down.

Verse 2
11
1:09–1:17
✓ LEARNED
Bars 33–36

Reel in & promenade both

…of what we found — hopeless hallelujah

Gather from the line back to hold, then step into a promenade walk.

🌟 Coaching: Draw her in on one smooth pull, then step out side-by-side — match stride length so you travel as one.

12
1:17–1:26
✓ LEARNED
Bars 37–40

Walk-around & underarm turn follow

Hope, this side of heaven

Hand-to-hand, walking around each other with a calm underarm turn.

🌟 Coaching: Keep one hand connected as you circle, eye contact the whole way. The turn is calm — let the music carry it.

Bridge
13
1:26–1:35
✓ LEARNED
Bars 41–44

Hand-to-hand walk-around both

You take my breath away

Keep one point of connection; circle each other, breathing room before the build.

🌟 Coaching: Breathing room: soft knees, easy steps, connected through the fingertips. Save energy — the lift is coming.

14
1:35–1:43
✓ LEARNED
Bars 45–48

Gather toward center both

Oh, my, my — taking me out

Draw back together toward the center, weight settling for the build.

🌟 Coaching: Close the distance deliberately; arrive at center with weight settled and frames ready. Set your feet for the build.

Build
15
1:43–1:52
✓ LEARNED
Bars 49–52

Push-pull tension both

I want you layin' me down…

Hand-to-hand push-pull; he sets his base as she commits her weight toward him.

🌟 Coaching: Play the give-and-take — he offers, she answers. Keep arms toned, not stiff, so the lead reads clearly.

Lift 1
16
1:52–1:58
✓ LEARNED
Bars 53–55

Rotating pickup lift lead★ SIGNATURE

instrumental swell — the lift

Feet off the floor, gown cascading, rotating together. SIGNATURE LIFT — soft surface first, lift with legs.

🌟 Coaching: Lift from the legs, not the back; Follow holds frame and spots the window — trust over tension.

17
1:58–2:02
✓ LEARNED
Bars 56–57

Soft landing embrace both

instrumental

Lower gently into an embrace and recover the frame.

🌟 Coaching: Lower her with control all the way down — no drop. Land into an embrace and share one breath before moving on.

Finale
18
2:02–2:07
✓ LEARNED
Bars 58–59

Open-arms presentation both

The angels up in the clouds

Side-by-side, big arms, chin up — present to the room.

🌟 Coaching: Big arms, open chest, chins up — present to the room like you mean it. Enjoy this 'we did it' beat.

19
2:07–2:11
✓ LEARNED
Bars 60–61

Solo spin away follow

…up in the clouds

Follow spins out and back, setting up the final lift.

🌟 Coaching: He sends, she spins with a clean spot and returns on balance. Keep it light and airy, setting up the finale.

20
2:11–2:15
✓ LEARNED
Bars 62–63

Climactic finale lift lead★ SIGNATURE

final swell

The big finale lift — the picture moment — then lower toward the embrace. SIGNATURE LIFT.

🌟 Coaching: This is the photo — sustain the height, chins up, breathe together; land soft and hold the gaze.

Outro
21
2:15–2:29
✓ LEARNED
Bars 64–70

Final embrace & bow both

outro — music resolves

Settle into the closing embrace and hold. The video's branding cards play over the end.

🌟 Coaching: Settle into the last embrace and HOLD — don't break early. Let the music resolve; that stillness is the picture.

The 4 things to actually drill

  • Turns & flourish (Bars 13–16, 0:26–0:35): follow spots the head or a slow turn gets dizzy; lead’s raised hand stays still.
  • The reach / open line (Bars 31–32, 1:05): a shape, not a step — commit, hold a beat, reel in smoothly.
  • Both lifts (Bars 53–55 · 1:52, and Bars 62–63 · 2:11): soft surface first; lead lifts with legs, follow keeps her frame tight, agree a verbal down-cue.
  • Floor travel (Bars 25–30, 0:52–1:05): mark start & end spots so you don’t drift off-camera.
